text.title: Token Organization Hierarchy and Naming Convention
text.summary: The three-tier token model (primitives, semantic, component) with a naming convention and examples.

rule.statement: Organize all tokens into exactly three tiers; never reference a primitive token directly from a component.
rule.rationale: Skipping the semantic layer collapses theming into a global search-and-replace; the semantic tier is the only place where "meaning" lives and can be swapped per theme.

list.item: Primitives — raw values with no semantic meaning: `color.primitive.blue-500`, `spacing.4`, `font-size.16`. Never consumed directly by components.
Semantic — role-based aliases that point to primitives: `color.surface.primary`, `color.text.danger`, `spacing.component.button`. Used in component styles.
Component — scoped overrides for components that genuinely deviate from the semantic layer: `button.padding.vertical`. Use sparingly; most components should consume only semantic tokens.

rule.statement: Name tokens using the pattern `{category}.{property}.{variant}.{state}` with all segments in kebab-case.
rule.rationale: Self-documenting names eliminate the need for external lookup; `color.surface.primary.hover` conveys category, property, variant, and state without a legend.

example.note: Raw-value names and camelCase break discovery and theming; component-plus-property-plus-state names in a single token cause component-token bloat.

rule.statement: Start with the minimum token set required to implement the design system; add tokens only when a genuine gap exists, not speculatively.
rule.rationale: Token bloat reduces discoverability and defeats the systematization purpose; every token without a consumer is maintenance debt.

list.item: Audit for duplicates before adding: does a token with the same value and role already exist?
Document why each token exists in its `$description`; tokens without documented purpose are candidates for deletion.
Version-control token changes and communicate renames with deprecation aliases before removal.
